title: WOS API Actor
description: Public API resource schema for WOS actors. An Actor is the addressable representation of a principal in the identity/governance subsystem (VISION §V). The four principal classes are normative and closed: `human | service-account | workload | support`. Identity details live here once and every other api/*.schema.json references actors via the `ActorRef` URN string from `_common.schema.json` (ADR 0082 D-9). This schema is greenfield — the actor resource (not just a reference) is authored as the canonical home of identity-display state used by every consumer. Authentication credentials, key material, attestation chain, and session lifecycle are NOT exposed here: actor-attestation is tracked separately under PLN-0381 (stack identity-attestation ADR, TBD number) and remains out of scope for this domain.

PrincipalClass.description: Closed VISION §V principal-class taxonomy. `human` = end-user with a personal identity (caseworker, applicant, reviewer). `service-account` = non-human service identity owned by an organization for routine automated tasks. `workload` = service-to-service identity for inter-layer transport (formspec-server / wos-server / trellis-store) — required, never API-key per VISION §V. `support` = reason-coded, time-boxed, audit-by-default break-glass identity distinct from staff. New classes require a major schema bump; vendor extension is intentionally not offered because principal-class semantics are normative.

ActorStatus.description: Closed lifecycle status of the actor record. `active` = principal may authenticate and act. `suspended` = principal is temporarily blocked; existing tokens are revoked but the record retains scope membership. `retired` = principal is permanently decommissioned; the record is preserved for historical references but cannot authenticate. Closed enum with no extension seam; new states require a schema major bump.

ActorScope.description: Closed scope-membership tuple for the actor (VISION §V scope hierarchy: `Tenant -> Organization -> Workspace -> Environment`). The shape is authored here so consumers have a stable contract; the load-bearing semantics are settled by ADR 0068 D-2 / D-3 (tenant and scope composition) which is currently *Proposed* — when ADR 0068 promotes to Accepted, this object becomes the wire form scope is reported in. Until then, server implementations MAY return only the fields they have settled.

tenant.description: Tenant identifier — outermost hosting/commercial boundary (VISION §V; ADR 0068 D-1 (Proposed)). Hosting-tier identity, not user-visible.

organization.description: Organization identifier — product-ownership boundary where forms, cases, and members live (VISION §V; ADR 0068 D-1 (Proposed)). Object-ownership and deletion-follows-org invariants tie retention to this id, not to billing state.

workspace.description: Optional workspace identifier — sub-scope inside an organization (VISION §V). Omitted when the actor is bound at the organization level.

environment.description: Closed deployment environment (VISION §V). `sandbox` = dev/test; `staging` = pre-prod; `prod` = live. New environments require a major schema bump.

Actor.description: Public WOS actor resource. The addressable shape of a principal in the identity/governance subsystem. Carries identity-display state used by every consumer (notification feeds, correspondence messages, governance reviews, audit timelines) so those consumers can resolve an `ActorRef` URN to a human-readable name without each implementing its own actor cache. Authentication credentials, attestation chain, and session lifecycle are not exposed here — see PLN-0381 (actor-attestation, separate ADR).

id.description: Stable public URN for this actor. The entity-type segment MUST be `actor`.

principalClass.description: Closed principal-class taxonomy from VISION §V. MUST equal the principal-class segment of the actor's `ActorRef` (the URN-form alias derived from this resource).

displayName.description: Human-readable display name suitable for UI rendering, audit timelines, and correspondence summaries. Open prose: distinct names for distinct actors are a UX concern, not a contract concern.

status.description: Closed lifecycle status of the actor record.

scope.description: Scope-membership tuple — Tenant / Organization / Workspace / Environment per VISION §V. Shape gates on ADR 0068 D-2 (Proposed); consumers tolerate the field's absence on early-deployment servers. When present the server MUST report tenant, organization, and environment; workspace is optional.

actorRef.description: URN-form alias derived from this resource (`actor:<principalClass>:<id-suffix>`). Consumers that already hold an `ActorRef` URN dereference it via this resource; consumers that hold the resource use this field as the canonical reference shape. The principal-class segment of the URN MUST equal `principalClass`.

createdAt.description: UTC RFC 3339 timestamp when the actor record was created in the identity/governance subsystem.

retiredAt.description: UTC RFC 3339 timestamp when the actor was retired. Present iff `status` is `retired`.

principalClass.description: When present, restrict results to actors of this principal class.

status.description: When present, restrict results to actors in this lifecycle status.

cursor.description: Opaque resume token returned by the previous page (ADR 0082 D-7).

limit.description: Maximum number of actors requested for this page (ADR 0082 D-7).

ActorPage.description: Cursor-paginated actor page (ADR 0082 D-7). Default ordering is `createdAt` ascending so paginating clients observe a stable monotone walk.

cursor.description: Opaque token for the next page. Omitted when hasMore is false.

hasMore.description: True when another page is available from the server.
